news_summary: Innovative tiny 'metajets' harnessing light to steer sails offer promising advancements for future interstellar travel.
news_summary_fr: De minuscules « métajets » innovants, qui exploitent la lumière pour diriger les voiles, ouvrent des perspectives prometteuses pour les futurs voyages interstellaires.
news_summary_es: Los innovadores «metajets» de tamaño minúsculo, que aprovechan la luz para dirigir las velas, ofrecen avances prometedores para los futuros viajes interestelares.

articles.title: Tiny 'metajets' could use light to steer sails for interstellar travel
articles.summary: Minuscule silicon wafers propelled by lasers could be used to steer light sails, helping them travel beyond the solar system
articles.content:
Minuscule silicon wafers propelled by lasers could be used to steer light sails, helping them travel beyond the solar system

An artist’s impression of a light sail RICHARD BIZLEY/SCIENCE PHOTO LIBRARY

Interstellar travel propelled by light just got one step closer. Light sails, which are huge sheets pushed along by light that bounces off of them, may be the best way to travel enormous distances through space, and now we may have a way to steer them.

“We knew already that any light or laser can impart momentum transfer, but now we can control the direction as well,” says Kaushik Kudtarkar at Texas A&M University. He and his colleagues created a tiny device called a metajet that uses refraction of light, not just reflection, to move in more than one direction at once.

The device is a material called a metasurface, an extremely thin sheet textured to manipulate light. In this case, the researchers flipped that on its head, using the light to manipulate the metasurface. A series of tiny pillars on the material steers the light that hits it, with the size and pattern of the pillars controlling the strength and direction of the momentum that the light imparts on the entire device as it moves through. The whole thing is about 0.01 millimetres across.

To test it, the researchers dropped the silicon device in water and shone a laser on it, watching it with a microscope to track its motion. They found that the metajet both levitated and moved horizontally, reaching a maximum speed of about 0.07 millimetres per second.

The metajet moving forwards, captured every 10 seconds Kaushik Kudtarkar et al. 2026

“Now that we know about the forces on this device, you can change the metasurface design and then you can steer it in any way you want,” says Kudtarkar. There are metasurfaces that change their shapes over time, and such a material could be used on light sails to steer them through space, he says.

Subscriber-only newsletter Sign up to Lost in Space-Time Untangle mind-bending physics, maths and the weirdness of reality with our monthly, special-guest-written newsletter. Sign up to newsletter

“For space, you can expand it, but you can keep it the same size and use it for biomedical applications as well: these devices could literally push drugs to a specific location,” says Kudtarkar. Lasers can already be used to do this, but their heat can damage the molecules, and with metajets, the drugs would not be directly exposed to the heat and light of a laser beam.

The researchers are now looking to make their device work with different wavelengths of light, especially the broad spectrum of sunlight to make them more compatible with the type of light sail that would be used for space travel. “It’s all a bit sci-fi,” says Kudtarkar.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant technological breakthrough in steering light sails for interstellar travel using tiny metajets, which could enable controlled propulsion beyond the solar system. This advancement has broad implications for space exploration and also potential biomedical applications, demonstrating a positive real-world impact with meaningful scope and sufficient detail.
articles.personality_title: Tiny metajets use light to steer sails for deep space travel
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists want to travel beyond our solar system using light sails. These sails are large, thin sheets pushed by light or lasers to move through space without fuel.

**What happened** – Researchers at Texas A&M University created tiny devices called metajets made from silicon wafers. These metajets use patterns of tiny pillars to bend light and change its direction, allowing the device to move in different ways when hit by a laser. They tested the metajets by shining lasers on them in water and saw the devices move and levitate.

**Impact** – This discovery shows that light sails could be steered more precisely by changing the design of their surfaces. This is important because it means spacecraft could be controlled better during long space trips. The technology could also help in medicine by moving drugs to exact places inside the body without harming them with laser heat.

**What's next step** – The researchers plan to make metajets that work with many kinds of light, especially sunlight, which would be useful for real space missions. They also want to explore materials that change shape to steer sails in different directions.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Tiny metajets that steer light sails using lasers bring us closer to controlled interstellar travel and new medical uses.
articles.personality_title_fr: De minuscules métajets utilisent la lumière pour diriger des voiles vers l’espace lointain
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les scientifiques souhaitent voyager au-delà de notre système solaire en utilisant des voiles lumineuses. Ces voiles sont de grandes feuilles fines poussées par la lumière ou les lasers pour se déplacer dans l’espace sans carburant.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs de l’université Texas A&M ont créé de minuscules dispositifs appelés métajets, fabriqués en silicium. Ces métajets utilisent des motifs de petits piliers pour dévier la lumière et changer sa direction, ce qui permet au dispositif de se déplacer de différentes façons lorsqu’il est frappé par un laser. Ils ont testé les métajets en les plaçant dans l’eau et en les éclairant avec un laser, observant leur mouvement et leur lévitation.

**Impact** – Cette découverte montre que les voiles lumineuses pourraient être mieux dirigées en modifiant la conception de leurs surfaces. C’est important car cela signifie que les engins spatiaux pourraient être mieux contrôlés lors de longs voyages dans l’espace. Cette technologie pourrait aussi aider en médecine en déplaçant des médicaments vers des endroits précis dans le corps sans les abîmer avec la chaleur du laser.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs prévoient de fabriquer des métajets fonctionnant avec plusieurs types de lumière, surtout la lumière du soleil, utile pour les missions spatiales réelles. Ils souhaitent aussi explorer des matériaux qui changent de forme pour diriger les voiles dans différentes directions.

**Phrase clé** – De minuscules métajets qui dirigent les voiles lumineuses avec des lasers rapprochent les voyages interstellaires contrôlés et de nouvelles applications médicales.
articles.personality_title_es: Pequeños metajets usan luz para dirigir velas en viajes espaciales profundos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los científicos quieren viajar más allá de nuestro sistema solar usando velas de luz. Estas velas son grandes láminas delgadas que se mueven en el espacio sin combustible, empujadas por la luz o láseres.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad Texas A&M crearon pequeños dispositivos llamados metajets hechos de obleas de silicio. Estos metajets usan patrones de pequeños pilares para doblar la luz y cambiar su dirección, permitiendo que el dispositivo se mueva de diferentes formas cuando un láser lo ilumina. Probaron los metajets en agua y vieron que se movían y levitaban.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento muestra que las velas de luz podrían dirigirse mejor cambiando el diseño de sus superficies. Esto es importante porque significa que las naves espaciales podrían controlarse mejor en viajes largos. La tecnología también podría ayudar en medicina moviendo medicamentos a lugares específicos del cuerpo sin dañarlos con el calor del láser.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores planean hacer metajets que funcionen con diferentes tipos de luz, especialmente la luz solar, útil para misiones espaciales reales. También quieren explorar materiales que cambien de forma para dirigir las velas en distintas direcciones.

**Frase clave** – Pequeños metajets que dirigen velas de luz con láseres acercan los viajes interestelares controlados y nuevas aplicaciones médicas.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a delicate, translucent silicon wafer patterned with tiny pillar-like structures, gently floating in a softly glowing beam of light that both lifts and steers it forward through a calm, abstract cosmic background, evoking the concept of light-driven interstellar travel and precise momentum control.
